any>(toolName: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): T {\n  const token = getToken();\n\n  const body = JSON.stringify({\n    jsonrpc: \"2.0\",\n    method: \"tools/call\",\n    params: { name: Error(response.error.message);\n\n  // MCP tool results come as content array with text fields\n  const content = response.result?.content;\n  if (content && content[0]?.text) {\n    return const response = JSON.parse(jsonStr);","originalFile":"import { execFileSync } from \"node:child_process\";\n\nconst MCP_URL = \"https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/\";\n\nfunction getToken(): string {\n  const token = process.env.GH_TOKEN;\n  if (!token) throw new Error(\"GH_TOKEN environment variable is required\");\n  return token;\n}\n\n/** Call a tool on the GitHub MCP server via HTTP and return parsed result. */\nexport function callMcpTool<T = any>(toolName: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): T {\n  const token = getToken();\n\n  const body = JSON.stringify({\n    jsonrpc: \"2.0\",\n    method: \"tools/call\",\n    params: { name: toolName, arguments: args },\n    id: 1,\n  });\n\n  const result = execFileSync(\"curl\", [\n    \"-s\", \"-X\", \"POST\", MCP_URL,\n    \"-H\", `Authorization: Bearer ${token}`,\n    \"-H\", \"Content-Type: application/json\",\n    \"-d\", body,\n  ], { encoding: \"utf-8\", timeout: 30000 });\n\n  const response = JSON.parse(result);\n  if (response.error) throw new Error(response.error.message);\n\n  // MCP tool results come as content array with text fields\n  const content = response.result?.content;\n  if (content && content[0]?.text) {\n    return JSON.parse(content[0].text) as T;\n  }\n  return response.result as T;\n}\n\n/** Call the GitHub REST API directly via curl. */\nexport function githubApi<T = any>(path: string): T {\n  const token = getToken();\n\n  const result = execFileSync(\"curl\", [\n    \"-s\",\n    \"-H\", `Authorization: Bearer ${token}`,\n    \"-H\", \"Accept: {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UUevUZ19hJDWo4qRJqtKTa","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Tools\n     2→\n     3→You have a TypeScript library at `./gh-code/` that wraps GitHub operations\n     4→as typed functions. Use it by writing a `.ts` script and running it with `npx tsx`.\n     5→\n     6→IMPORTANT: Do NOT use the `gh` CLI or `curl` to call the GitHub API directly.\n     7→You must use the `./gh-code/` library exclusively for all GitHub operations.\n     8→\n     9→## Available modules\n    10→\n    11→- `./gh-code/issues` — listIssues, viewIssue, listComments\n    12→- `./gh-code/prs` — viewPR, listChecks\n    13→- `./gh-code/releases` — listReleases, viewRelease\n    14→- `./gh-code/repo` — viewRepo\n    15→- `./gh-code/labels` — listLabels\n    16→- `./gh-code/runs` — listRuns, viewRun\n    17→\n    18→## Usage pattern\n    19→\n    20→Write a TypeScript file, then run it:\n    21→\n    22→```ts\n    23→import { listIssues } from \"./gh-code/issues\";\n    24→const issues = listIssues(\"openclaw/openclaw\", { state: \"open\", limit: 5 });\n    25→console.log(JSON.stringify(issues, null, 2));\n    26→```\n    27→\n    28→Then execute: `npx tsx script.ts`\n    29→\n    30→You can compose multiple operations in a single script to minimize round trips.\n    31→Filter and process data in code before logging results.\n    32→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"351c178c-36b0-4cb1-a474-a8cf4527035e","uuid":"aabac8e2-9b0b-4f34-920a-83284c7acec3","timestamp":"2026-03-21T18:40:46.081Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"./bench/results/mcp-with-code-mode/pr_then_checks/run2/workspace/AGENTS.md","content":"# Tools\n\nYou have a TypeScript library at `./gh-code/` that wraps GitHub operations\nas typed functions. Use it by writing a `.ts` script and running it with `npx tsx`.\n\nIMPORTANT: Do NOT use the `gh` CLI or `curl` to call the GitHub API directly.\nYou must use the `./gh-code/` library exclusively for all GitHub operations.\n\n## Available modules\n\n- `./gh-code/issues` — listIssues, viewIssue, listComments\n- `./gh-code/prs` — viewPR, listChecks\n- `./gh-code/releases` — listReleases, viewRelease\n- `./gh-code/repo` — viewRepo\n- `./gh-code/labels` — listLabels\n- `./gh-code/runs` — listRuns, viewRun\n\n## Usage pattern\n\nWrite a TypeScript file, then run it:\n\n```ts\nimport { listIssues } from \"./gh-code/issues\";\nconst issues = listIssues(\"openclaw/openclaw\", { state: \"open\", limit: 5 });\nconsole.log(JSON.stringify(issues, null, 2));\n```\n\nThen execute: `npx tsx script.ts`\n\nYou can compose multiple operations in a single script to minimize round trips.\nFilter and process data in code before logging results.\n","numLines":32,"startLine":1,"totalLines":32}}}

raw.author?.login ?? \"\",\n    isDraft: raw.draft ?? raw.isDraft ?? false,\n    mergedAt: raw.merged_at ?? raw.mergedAt ?? null,\n  };\n}","originalFile":"import { callMcpTool, githubApi } from \"./exec.js\";\n\nexport interface PR {\n  number: number;\n  title: string;\n  state: string;\n  author: string;\n  isDraft: boolean;\n  mergedAt: string | null;\n}\n\nexport interface CheckSummary {\n  name: string;\n  status: string;\n  conclusion: string;\n}\n\n/** View a single pull request. */\nexport function viewPR(repo: string, number: number): PR {\n  const [owner, name] = repo.split(\"/\");\n  const raw = callMcpTool<any>(\"pull_request_read\", { owner, repo: name, pull_number: number });\n  return {\n    number: raw.number,\n    title: raw.title,\n    state: raw.state,\n    author: raw.user?.login ?? raw.author?.login ?? \"\",\n    isDraft: raw.draft ?? raw.isDraft ?? false,\n    mergedAt: raw.merged_at ?? raw.mergedAt ?? null,\n  };\n}\n\n/** List status checks for a pull request. */\nexport function listChecks(repo: string, prNumber: number): CheckSummary[] {\n  const [owner, name] = repo.split(\"/\");\n  // Get the PR head SHA first, then fetch check runs\n  const pr = githubApi<{ head: { sha: string } }>(`/repos/${owner}/${name}/pulls/${prNumber}`);\n  const raw = githubApi<{\n    check_runs: Array<{\n      name: string;\n      status: string;\n      conclusion: string | null;\n    }>;\n  }>(`/repos/${owner}/${name}/commits/${pr.head.sha}/check-runs`);\n  return (raw.check_runs ??
